Fund Raise / QIP / NCD.headline: Outcome of the meeting of the Right Issue Committee of Sadhana Nitro Chem Limited (the "Company") held on 12th March 2026 at 7.00 P.M
Fund Raise / QIP / NCD.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, To, BSE Limited National Stock Exchange India Limited 1st Floor, P. J Towers, Exchange Plaza, C-1, Block G, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E), Mumbai – 400001 Mumbai – 400051 Scrip Code: 506642 Symbol: SADHNANIQ Sub: Outcome of the meeting of Rights Issue Committee of Sadhana Nitro Chem Limited held on March 12, 2026 Ref: Allotment of Equity Shares under Right Issue of Sadhana Nitro Chem Limited (“the Company”) Dear Sir/ Madam, The Empowered Rights Issue Committee (“RIC”), duly authorised by the Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held today, i.e., Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 7:00 p.m., has inter alia considered and approved the allotment of equity shares pursuant to the Rights Issue of the Company. In relation to the aforesaid Rights Issue and pursuant to the finalization of the Basis of Allotment of the Rights Issue in consultation with the Registrar to the Issue and BSE Limited, the Designated Stock Exchange , the RIC at its meeting held on March 12, 2026 has inter alia passed the resolution for approving the allotment of 263,52,83,328 fully paid up Equity Shares at an issue price of ₹ 1/- per Equity Share to the eligible equity shareholders or applicants (renouncees), as the case may be pursuant to the Rights Issue. Further, 5128 (Five Thousand One Hundred and Twenty Eight) Rights Equity Shares have been kept in abeyance pending regulatory/ other clearances, as may be applicable. Accordingly, the paid-up equity share capital of the Company has been increased from Rs. 32,94,11,057 to Rs. 2,96,46,94,385 by addition of 263,52,83,328 equity shares. The said fully paid-up Equity Shares allotted to eligible equity shareholders or applicants (renouncees), as the case may be, shall rank pari- passu in all respect with the existing fully paid-up Equity Shares of the Company. This disclosure is made in terms of Regulation 30 and 42 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. We reques

Mumbai – 400 051. BSE Scrip Code: 540776 NSE Symbol: 5PAISA Dear Sir / Madam, Sub: Submission of Letter of Offer for Rights Issue of fully paid-up equity shares of face value of ₹ 10 (“Equity Shares”) of 5paisa Capital Limited (the “Company”) This is in continuation to our earlier announcements dated February 24, 2026 and March 11, 2026, wherein the Company had proposed to undertake an issuance of 15,627,419 fully paid-up Equity Shares (“Rights Equity Shares”) at an issue price of ₹ 300/- per Rights Equity Share (including a premium of ₹ 290/- per Rights Equity Share), aggregating up to ₹ 4,688.23 million to the eligible equity shareholders of the Company in the ratio of 1 (One) Rights Equity Shares for every 2 (Two) fully paid-up Equity Shares held by eligible shareholders on the record date i.e., Tuesday, March 17, 2026. In this regard, please find enclosed the soft copy of letter of offer dated March 12, 2026 (“Letter of Offer”) which was approved by the Board of Directors of the Company. The Company is submitting the Letter of Offer with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (“SEBI”), BSE Limited and National Stock Exchange of India Limited on March 12, 2026, in compliance with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, as amended, and the SEBI Circular bearing no. (HO/49/14/14(2)2026-CFD-POD2/I/4518/2026) dated February 09, 2026. We kindly request you to take the above on record. Thanking you, For 5paisa Capital Limited ______________________ Charvi Panchmatia Company Secretary & Compliance Officer ICSI Membership No.: A49189 Email ID: csteam@5paisa.com 5paisa Capital Limited Registered Office: IIFL House, Sun In

BSE CODE: 524208 NSE Symbol : AARTIIND Dear Sir / Madam, Sub: Press Release Ref: Regulation 30 of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015 Pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, please find enclosed herewith Copy of Press Release titled “Aarti Industries Limited Secures USD 150 Million Medium-Term Supply Contract with Global Agrochemical Major”. Kindly take the same on record. Thanking You, Yours faithfully, FOR AARTI INDUSTRIES LIMITED RAJ SARRAF COMPANY SECRETARY ICSI M. NO. A15526 Encl.: As above.Aarti Industries Limited Secures USD 150 Million Medium-Term Supply Contract with Global Agrochemical Major Mumbai, India, 12th March, 2026: Aarti Industries Limited (AIL), a leading global manufacturer of speciality chemicals, has entered into a multi-year supply agreement with a top global agrochemical innovator for the supply of a critical agrochemical intermediate used in crop protection formulations. This agreement elevates the current annual engagement to a structured supply contract through 31st March 2030, with a significant increase in volumes. The agreement is expected to generate revenue of approximately USD 150 million over the contract period, providing AIL with enhanced revenue visibility in the medium to long term. Under the terms of the agreement, AIL will manufacture and supply the intermediate, leveraging its integrated, scalable manufacturing platform, deep process chemistry expertise, and established global regulatory compliance framework. Acquisitions & M&A.headline: Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, please find attached herewith a General Announcement.
Acquisitions & M&A.pdf_text: To Date: 12.03.2026 BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Dalal Street, Mumbai-400001 Maharashtra, India (Scrip Code: 544151) Sub: Chatha Foods Operational Update Amid Recent LPG Supply Concerns Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are enclosing herewith a General Announcement issued by the Company regarding the potential LPG supply constraints in India arising from global geopolitical developments. The detailed communication in this regard is enclosed as Annexure A. This communication is being shared for the information of the stakeholders of the Company. We request you to kindly take the announcement on record. The above information is also being available on the Company’s website at https://cfpl.net.in/. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For Chatha Foods Limited Priyanka Oberoi Company Secretary & Compliance Officer(Annexure A) General Announcement: Chatha Foods Operational Update Amid Recent LPG Supply Concerns In light of the ongoing discussions around potential LPG supply constraints in India arising from global geopolitical developments, Chatha Foods Limited would like to inform its stakeholders regarding the Company’s manufacturing operations. The Company’s production processes are not dependent on LPG as a primary energy source. Our facilities are designed to operate through electricity and diesel-powered systems, enabling continuity of operations independent of LPG availability. Accordingly, any potential disruption or shortage in LPG supply is not expected to have a material impact on the Company’s manufacturing activities at this time. Chatha Foods continues to operate normally and remains focused on maintaining consistent production and supply to its partners and customers. The Company will continue to monitor the broader energy supply environment and take appropriate steps, where necessary, to ensure operational stability. ****

Regulatory & BSE Queries.pdf_text: March 12, 2026 To, To, National Stock Exchange of India Limited BSE Limited Listing Department, The Department of Corporate Services Exchange Plaza, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Bandra (E), Mumbai – 400 051 Mumbai 400 001 Ref Symbol: VASCONEQ Ref: Scrip Code: 533156 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub: Press Release on Secures ₹115.90 Crore Lotus Park Development Project in Ahmedabad We enclosed herewith a Press Release dated March 12, 2026, titles as “Vascon Engineers Secures ₹115.90 Crore Lotus Park Development Project in Ahmedabad” Please take the information on record. For Vascon Engineers Limited Neelam Piyush Pipada Company Secretary and Compliance Officer M No.: A31721Vascon Engineers Secures ₹115.90 Crore Lotus Park Development Project in Ahmedabad Mumbai, March 12, 2026: Vascon Engineers Ltd. (VASCONEQ), one of the leading EPC and Realty players with a legacy spanning over four decades, has received a Letter of Intent from Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation (AMC) for the Development of Lotus Park on 54,000 Sq. Mtrs, South West Zone, under the Building Project of Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation, Ahmedabad. The project is valued at ₹115.90 crore and it is going to be executed on an EPC basis, with a completion timeline of 24 months from the date of issuance of the work order. This order further strengthens Vascon's growing order book and reinforces its track record of winning prestigious municipal infrastructure mandates across India. This landmark contract strengthens Vascon's order book, which currently stands at ₹2,825 crore- representing a healthy 2.8x coverage of FY25 EPC revenues. With the ability to execute approximately 8 million square feet annually and currently operating at 90% utilization, supported by 800+ strong project and engineering team, Vascon is well-positioned to deliver the upcoming infrastructure and EPC Projects. Soni Company Secretary and Compliance Officer Membership No. FCS7063 Encl.: As aboveZydus expands companion diagnostics portfolio with the launch of AI-powered Continuous Glucose Monitor DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM are designed for diabetic, Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD), and post- transplant patients who require continuous glycaemic surveillance and integrated remote care The next-gen CGM is paired with AI-powered analytics and a dedicated clinician remote- monitoring dashboard which is integrated with GoodFlipTM app designed by TatvaCareTM Ahmedabad, 12 March, 2026 Zydus Lifesciences Limited (“Zydus”), an innovation-led global life sciences company, today announced its new offering companion diagnostics portfolio. The company will be launching DiasensTM and GlucoLiveTM, next-generation Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) devices that combine artificial intelligence- powered insights, and integrated remote care capabilities. The CGM system is designed to monitor glucose with an integrated AI layer to provide analytics and enable a closed-loop care ecosystem connecting patients, caregivers, and clinicians in real time. Customers who prefers the TVS Orbiter V1 without BaaS, can purchase it at ₹84,500 (ex-showroom Delhi, inclusive of PM e-Drive). Credit Ratings.raw_headline: Submission of Press Release- Cricket Kit Distribution Ceremony at SPG-Rose Merc Cricket Academy.
Credit Ratings.headline: Submission of Press Release- Cricket Kit Distribution Ceremony at SPG-Rose Merc Cricket Academy.
Credit Ratings.pdf_text: Date: March 12th 2026 To, The Corporate Relations Department, Bombay Stock Exchange Limited, P J Tower, Dalal Street, Fort, Mumbai – 400001 BSE Scrip Code: 512115 Scrip ID: ROSEMER Subject: Submission of Press Release – “Cricket Kit Distribution Ceremony at SPG – Rose Merc Cricket Academy”. Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are pleased to submit herewith a press release titled “Rose Merc Limited Successfully Hosts Cricket Kit Distribution Ceremony at SPG – Rose Merc Cricket Academy.” The event was held on 11 March 2026 at Shivaji Park Gymkhana and celebrated young cricketers from the Under- 14, Under-16, and Under-19 categories of the SPG – Rose Merc Cricket Academy. The ceremony was graced by prominent cricketers including Mr. Shreyas Iyer, Mr. Siddhesh Lad and former India cricketer Mr. Pravin Amre, who interacted with the young trainees and distributed cricket kits. The initiative reflects the Company’s continued commitment to promoting grassroots sports development and nurturing young cricketing talent through its collaboration with Shivaji Park Gymkhana. The press release is enclosed for your information and records. This is for your information and records. Thanking You, Yours Faithfully, For ROSE MERC LIMITED Vaishali Parkar Kumar Managing Director DIN: 09159108 Encl.: Press Release“An inspiring moment for our young trainees as they pose with Mr. Sachin Deshpande, Ms. Eshwari Shelatkar - Executive Director, Rose Merc Ltd. and Director, Moda Orama Ventures Pvt. Ltd, Mr. Purvesh Shelatkar - Executive Director, Rose Merc Ltd., Ms. Harshee Haria – Media Head, Rose Merc Ltd. and Director, Navi Mumbai Premier League Pvt. Ltd., and joining them are Indian international cricketer Mr. Shreyas Iyer, Mumbai cricketer Mr. Siddhesh Lad, and Mr. Pravin Amre- former India cricketer and president of Shivaji Park gymkhana. Together, we celebrate the spirit of youth and ambition, paving

Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we would like to inform that Mrs. Mullapudi Kalyani Sudha, being the spouse of Mr. Mullapudi Atchuta Ramarao, promoter of the Company, forms part of the “Promoter Group” of the Company in terms of the applicable provisions of the SEBI Regulations. Management Changes.raw_headline: Corrigendum To The Notice Of The Extra-Ordinary General Meeting
Management Changes.headline: Corrigendum To The Notice Of The Extra-Ordinary General Meeting
Management Changes.pdf_text: Date: 12-03-2026 To, Corporate Relationship Department BSE Limited, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai - 400 001 Sub: Covering Letter for Corrigendum I to the EGM Notice dated March 02, 2026 Scrip Code: 538579 | ISIN: INE641Q01019 Dear Sirs/Madam, This is with reference to the Notice of the Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) of M/s Zinema Media and Entertainment Limited scheduled to be held on Saturday, March 28, 2026, at 03.30 P.M. through Video Conferencing (“VC”)/Other Audio Visual Means (“OAVM”). The Company is issuing the enclosed Corrigendum I to provide a necessary update to the original Notice. Specifically, please be advised that the name of Mr. C.R. Vasudevan has been removed from the list of proposed allottees under Item No. 1 regarding the preferential issue of equity shares. All other resolutions, including the issuance of sweat equity shares to Directors (Item No. 2), the acquisition of equity stake in M/s Beontyme Technologies Private Limited (Item No. 3), preferential issue of equity stake in M/s Tulsea Media Private Limited (Item No. 4), and the increase in authorized share capital (Item No. 5), remain unchanged and as stated in the original Notice.  Integration: This Corrigendum should be read as an integral part of the EGM Notice dated March 02, 2026.  E-Voting: The remote e-voting period remains from Wednesday, March 25, 2026 (09:00 A.M.) to Friday, March 27, 2026 (05:00 P.M.). The above documents are also available on the Company's website i.e. https://zinema.co.in, on the website of the Stock Exchanges i.e. BSE Limited (BSE) www.bseindia.com respectively, and the website of National Securities Depository Limited (NSDL) https://www.evoting.nsdl.com. You are requested to kindly take the above information on your records. ZINEMA MEDIA & ENTERTAINMENT LIMITED Registered Office – New Door Number 57, Old Door No.57, Sathya dev Avenue MRC Nagar, Raja Annamalaipuram, Raja Annamalaipuram, Chennai: 600028 CIN: L24241TN1984PLC096136 Email

Appointment of Mr. Shalin Harshadbhai Patel (DIN: 08214933) as an Additional Non- Executive, Independent Director of the Company: Based on the recommendation of the Nomination and Remuneration Committee and further subject to the approval of Shareholders of the Company, the Board of Directors had appointed Mr. Shalin Harshadbhai Patel (DIN: 08214933) as an Additional Director (Non-Executive and Independent Director) of the Company and is recommended to be appointed as the Independent Director, not be liable to retire by rotation. Management Changes.raw_headline: Reply to the clarification sought with reference to the movement in volume
Management Changes.headline: Reply to the clarification sought with reference to the movement in volume
Management Changes.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To BSE Surveillance BSE Ltd P J Towers Dalal Street Mumbai - 400 001 Scrip Code: 544485 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub: Reply to the Clarification sought on movement in price of the shares across Exchanges of Shares of the Company-Regulation 30 of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015. Ref.: Your Email reference No. L/SURV/ONL/PV/SG/ 2025-2026 / 971 dated March 12, 2026 With reference to your above-mentioned email in respect of clarification sought on price movement of our scrip, kindly note that in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, we hereby inform you that there is no pending information or announcement from the company which is/are price sensitive that could have led to significant movement in the price behaviour of the Company's shares. Further, as on date our Company does not have any undisclosed/potentially price sensitive information/event /announcement, that is required to be disclosed to the stock exchange under Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, which in our opinion, may have a bearing on the price/volume of our Company's security. Therefore, we believe that the recent movement in price of our equity shares is purely due to market driven conditions. Further, we would like to assure you that Company will ensure stock exchange(s) are duly informed of all the information/ announcements required to be disclosed under applicable regulations, as and when required. We hope the above clarifies and request you to kindly take the same on your records. Thanking you, For Regaal Resources Limited Tinku Kumar Gupta Company Secretary and Compliance Officer

Resignation of Chief Financial Officer (“CFO”) of the Company The Board took note of the resignation of Mr. Vaibhav Ashok Rupnar (PAN: AGQPR4390G) from his role as CFO with effect from 04th March 2026. 2. Resignation of Mr. Anand Pravin Pande (DIN: 07245254) from the Directorship of the Company The Board took note of the resignation of Mr. Anand Pravin Pande (DIN: 07245254) from his role as a Non- Executive Director with effect from 04th March 2026. 3. Cessation of Whole-Time Company Secretary The Board took note of the resignation of CS Mohini Talhar (Membership No.: A62080) from the role as Whole- Time Company Secretary with effect from 04th March 2026. 4. Appointment of Whole-Time Company Secretary The Board of Directors of the Company, on the recommendation of Nomination and Remuneration Committee, at its meeting held on 11th March 2026, has approved the appointment of Ms. Na

Management Changes.subcategory: Award of Order / Receipt of Order
Management Changes.pdf_text: To, BSE Limited, Floor 25, P J Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400001 Script Code: 543963 Ref: Intimation with regards to “Shelter Pharma” secured order from First Vet Veterinary Medicines Trading LLC, UAE pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulation, 2015 Pursuant to regulation 30(4) of the SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulation, 2015, we are pleased to inform you that “Shelter Pharma Limited” secured a noteworthy order from First Vet Veterinary Medicines Trading LLC, UAE for Supply of veterinary / animal healthcare products total order worth 18,300 $. The details of the order as per SEBI is been annexed herewith this letter. Kindly take the note of above information on record and oblige. About Shelter Pharma Limited: Shelter Pharma Limited is a leading pharmaceutical and nutraceutical company known for its unwavering dedication to research, innovation, and quality. Founded with a vision to provide accessible and effective healthcare solutions, we have consistently delivered products that adhere to the highest industry standards. Our state-of-the-art facilities, world-class team of experts, and commitment to ethical practices have made us a trusted name in the industry. At Shelter Pharma Limited, our mission is to promote well-being and health through innovative, high-quality nutraceuticals. We take immense pride in being recognized for our commitment to producing safe and effective products that enhance the quality of life for individuals across the globe. Our passion for innovation drives us to develop nutraceutical products that empower individuals to lead healthier lives. We are guided by a strong code of ethics and a deep sense of responsibility to society. For any inquiries or additional information, please feel free to contact our Investor Relations team. Kindly take the above on record and oblige. Thanking You. FOR, SHELTER PHARMA LIMITED ___________________________________ MUSTAQIM N

Web: www.hdbfs.com Tel: 022 – 4911 6350 Fax: 022 – 4911 6666 CIN: L65993GJ2007PLC051028 Email: investorcommunications@hdbfs.com HDB/SLC/2026/1448 March 12, 2026 To, To, Listing Compliance Department Listing Compliance Department National Stock Exchange of India Limited BSE Limited Exchange Plaza, Plot No C/1, Block G, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Bandra (East), Dalal Street, Fort, Mumbai - 400051 Mumbai - 400001 Scrip Code: HDBFS Scrip Code: 544429 Dear Sir / Madam, Sub.: Disclosure under Regulation 30 & 51 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (SEBI Listing Regulations) – Change in Senior Management Personnel We wish to inform you that based on the recommendations of the Nomination and Remuneration Committee, the Board of Directors of the Company, has approved the appointment of Mr. Akash Bararia as the Head – Consumer Finance of the Company on March 12, 2026 with immediate effect. Mr. Akash Bararia will be a Senior Management Personnel of the Company. The details as required in terms of disclosure under Regulation 30 read with Clauses 7 of Paragraph A of Part A of Schedule III to the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 read with SEBI Master Circular no. Towers, Bandra (E), Dalal Street Fort, Mumbai - 400051 Mumbai- 400001 SCRIP CODE: VARDMNPOLY SCRIP CODE: 514175 SUBJECT: INTIMATION OF BOARD MEETING Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 29 and other applicable regulations of SEBI (LODR) regulations, 2015, this is to inform you that the Meeting of the Board of Directors of the Company is scheduled to be held on Tuesday, 17" day of March, 2026 at the Registered Office of the Company i.e. Vardhman Park, Chandigarh Road, Ludhiana - 141123, inter alia, to consider and approve the fund raising by way of debt or issue of debentures or equity shares or any other instrument or security, including any other convertible securities, through preferential issue or through any other permissible mode and/ or combination thereof as may be considered appropriate, subject to such approvals as may be required including the approval of the membars and other statutory/ regulatory approvals, as applicable and other related matters. Board Decisions.pdf_text: March 12, 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Exchange Plaza, Bandra Kurla Complex, Dalal Street Bandra (East) Mumbai – 400 021 Mumbai – 400 051 Scrip Code: 500271 Symbol: MFSL Sub: Outcome of the meeting of the Board of Directors (“Board”) of Max Financial Services Limited (“Company”) pursuant to Regulation 30 of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“Listing Regulations”) Dear Sir/Madam, With reference to our letter dated March 7, 2026, and in accordance with Regulation 30 of the Listing Regulations, and other applicable provisions of law, we hereby inform you that the Board at its meeting held earlier in the day, i.e. Thursday, March 12, 2026, has inter-alia considered and approved the following: (i) Raising of funds by way of issuing fully paid-up equity shares (having face value of ₹2 each of the Company), other equity-based instruments or securities, (all of which are hereinafter referred to as “Securities”), in one or more tranches, by way of a qualified institutional placement or any other method or any combination thereof as may be permitted in accordance with the applicable provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, as amended from time to time, and all other applicable laws, subject to receipt of such necessary governmental/statutory/regulatory and other approvals as may be required, including the approval of the shareholders of the Company for an aggregate amount not exceeding ₹ 2,000 Crores (Indian Rupees Two Thousand Crores only) or an equivalent amount thereof (inclusive of any premium as may be fixed on such Securities). The proposed utilization of fund raise is primarily to meet the funding requirements of its material subsidiary company, viz., Axis Max Life Insurance Limited, for supporting its business growth and expansion pl

Detailed disclosure as required under Regulation 30 Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 and relevant circulars of SEBI including amendments thereon, are provided in the Annexure I enclosed to this Intimation. Request you to take the above information / details and enclosed documents on your records. Board Decisions.headline: Media Release - Aurum PropTech exits non-core Assets for INR 112 Crores, Builds War Chest to Scale AI Leadership.
Board Decisions.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, Listing Department Listing Department BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Bandra Kurla Complex Dalal Street, Fort Bandra East Mumbai-400 001 Mumbai – 400 051 BSE Scrip Code: 539289 NSE Symbol: AURUM Dear Sir/Madam, Sub.: Media Release - Aurum PropTech Exits non-core Assets for INR 112 Crores, Builds War Chest to Scale AI Led PropTech. Pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements), Regulations, 2015, please find enclosed a copy of the Media Release issued by the Company with regard to the captioned subject. The Board of Directors, at its meeting held today, i.e., March 12, 2026, has approved the sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 at Millennium Business Park, Navi Mumbai. The Media Release will also be available on the Company’s website at www.aurumproptech.in. You are requested to take the above on record. Thanking you. For Aurum PropTech Limited Sonia Jain Company Secretary & Compliance OfficerMEDIA RELEASE Aurum PropTech Exits non-core Assets for INR 112 Crores, Builds War Chest to Scale AI led PropTech Mumbai, India – March 12, 2026: Aurum PropTech Limited today announced that its Board of Directors has approved the sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 at Millennium Business Park, Navi Mumbai, for a total consideration of INR 112 Crores, approximately 15% above the valuation provided by the valuers. The transaction forms part of Aurum PropTech’s broader strategic shift toward AI-driven PropTech platforms, with the company committing significant capital toward building advanced artificial intelligence capabilities across the real estate ecosystem. The proceeds from the transaction will further strengthen Aurum’s growing AI investment pool and support the accelerated development of intelligent digital infrastructure for the real estate sector. The assets have a approximate book value of INR 27 Crores, and the transaction will generate a substantial profit, thereby enhancing the
Board Decisions.clean_headline: Aurum PropTech Board approves sale of Buildings Q5 and Q6 at Millennium Business Park, Navi Mumbai

Board Decisions.pdf_text: 12th March 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze JeeJeeBhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street, 5th Floor, Plot No. C-1, G Block, Mumbai - 400 001 Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E) Mumbai - 400 051 SCRIP CODE: 523367 SCRIP CODE: DCMSHRIRAM Sub : Outcome of Board Meeting dated 12th March 2026 Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (‘Listing Regulations’), we hereby inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company at its meeting held today i.e., 12th March 2026 has approved the following: - Equity Investment of upto Rs.87 crores, in one or more tranches, for minimum 26% equity stake in one or more Special Purpose Vehicles, to be created for setting up a Renewable Energy Power project for its Bharuch Plant; and - proposal for incurring capital expenditure of upto to Rs. 130 crores, towards related infrastructure development. This will enable the Company to obtain ~ 48 MW of additional Renewable Power supply (~ 30 MW round the clock at 75% Capacity Utilization Factor) for its Bharuch Plant, thereby taking the total RE power provision for the Bharuch Plant from the present peak of 50.4MW to 98.4MW. The indicative time line for completion of the above project will be around June 2027. The details required under Regulation 30 of SEBI Listing Regulations read with SEBI Circular No. HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 dated 30th January 2026, for the above investment proposal will be provided separately in due course, once details/other terms are finalised. The meeting of the Board of Directors of the Company commenced at 3.00 p.m. and concluded at 3.50 p.m. Kindly take the above information on record. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For DCM Shriram Limited (Deepak Gupta) Company Secretary & Compliance Officer

Warrant Conversions.headline: Press Release dated 12.03.2026
Warrant Conversions.pdf_text: PRESS RELEASE CAMIKARA BECOMES THE WORLD’S BEST INDIAN RUM. BEATS GLOBAL RUM BRANDS After Indian single malt whisky captured global attention, Indian rum is now stepping into the spotlight — with Camikara leading the charge th 12 March 2026, New Delhi - Over the past decade, Indian single malt whisky has transformed the country’s reputation in global spirits, challenging long-established whisky regions and earning international acclaim. Now, a similar shift may be unfolding in another category deeply rooted in India’s agricultural heritage — Rum. In a landmark moment for Indian spirits, Camikara — India’s first pure cane juice aged rum — has secured top honours at two of the world’s most respected rum competitions, signalling the emergence of India as a serious contender in premium rum. At the Global Rum & Cachaça Masters Awards 2026 in the United Kingdom, Camikara 8-Year- Old was awarded the prestigious ‘Master Medal’, the competition’s highest distinction. In the same competition, Camikara 3-Year-Old secured its second consecutive ‘Gold Medal’, making Camikarathe only Indian rum to win both Master and Gold in the same year. Recognition soon followed in the United States. At the Aged Rum category tasting conducted by The Fifty Best, Camikara 8YO received a ‘Double Gold’ Medal, a distinction reserved for spirits that receive unanimous top scores from all judges. In the International blend category, Camikara 3YO also secured ‘Gold’, reinforcing the brand’s consistency across both expressions and international tasting panels. Piccadily Agro Industries Ltd. Analyst / Investor Meets.headline: KPI Green Energy Limited has informed the exchange regarding Press Release titled "KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio'. ....
Analyst / Investor Meets.pdf_text: KPI/MAT/MAR/2026/757 Date: March 12, 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai - 400 001 Bandra (E), Mumbai - 400051 Scrip Code: 542323 Symbol: KPIGREEN Sub.: Press Release – KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are enclosing herewith a Press Release titled “KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio”. This portfolio highlights a robust and sustainable annuity revenue stream from long-term contracted projects under PPAs (many of them 25-year PPA’s), reinforcing the Company’s strategic transition toward an asset-backed IPP model complemented by its established EPC expertise. This is for your information and records. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For KPI Green Energy Limited Moh. Sohil Yusuf Dabhoya Whole Time Director DIN: 07112947For immediate release KPI Green Energy Limited energises 35 MWp to reach 589 MWp energised capacity out of 2.17 GWp IPP Portfolio KPI Green Energy Limited (“KPI Green” or “the Company”) has recently energised an additional 35 MWp of solar capacity, taking its total operational IPP capacity to 589 MWp. With projects currently in hand, the Company remains well on track to commission an additional 1,582 MWp in the near term, which will expand its total IPP portfolio to 2.17 GWp. This continued capacity addition further strengthens KPI Green’s position as a leading Independent Power Producer (IPP). It further reinforces the Company’s strategic transition toward an asset-backed Independent Power Produce

Appointment of Mr. Vinit Rameshchandra Mundra (DIN: 11597227) as an Additional Non- Executive, Independent Director of the Company: Based on the recommendation of the Nomination and Remuneration Committee and further subject to the approval of Shareholders of the Company, the Board of Directors had appointed Mr. Vinit Rameshchandra Mundra (DIN: 11597227) as an Additional Director (Non-Executive and Independent Director) of the Company and is recommended to be appointed as the Independent Director for a term of 5 (Five) consecutive years commencing from 12th March, 2026 till 11th March, 2031 (both days inclusive), not be liable to retire by rotation. Mr. Vinit Rameshchandra Mundra (DIN: 11597227) is not disqualified from being appointed as an Additional Director (Non-Executive and Independent Director) in terms of Section 164 of the Companies Act 2013 and have given his consent to act as an Additional Director (Non-Executive and Independent Director). The resolution relating to the appointment of S.R. MoUs & Agreements.pdf_text: Date: March 12, 2026 To, To, BSE Limited, National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai- 400001 Bandra (E), Mumbai- 400001 Scrip Code: 543714 Symbol: LANDMARK Sub.: Press Release – Landmark Cars Enhances Its Hyderabad Aftersales Network with a New Mahindra & Mahindra Workshop Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we are enclosing herewith a Press Release titled “Landmark Cars Enhances Its Hyderabad Aftersales Network with a New Mahindra & Mahindra Workshop”. Request you to please take the same on your record. Thanking You, Yours faithfully, For Landmark Cars Limited Amol Arvind Raje Company Secretary & Compliance Officer Mem. No.: A19459 Encl. as above Landmark Cars Limited CIN: L50100GJ2006PLC058553 | GSTIN: 24AABCL1862B1Z2 Registered Office: Landmark House, Opp. AEC, Near Gurudwara, S. G. Highway, Thaltej, Ahmedabad – 380059 Tel.: +91-7966185555 | Email: info@landmarkcars.in | Website: www.grouplandmark.inLandmark Cars Enhances Its Hyderabad Aftersales Network with a New Mahindra & Mahindra Workshop Thursday, 12th March 2026, Mumbai - Landmark Cars Limited (BSE: 543714 | NSE: LANDMARK), a leading premium automotive retail network in India, is set to expand its footprint in Hyderabad by opening a new Mahindra & Mahindra (‘M&M’) workshop in Kompally, Hyderabad. This workshop will help Landmark in catering to high demand witnessed in the existing two M&M workshops in Hyderabad. This workshop will be established in one of the wholly owned subsidiaries of Landmark Cars, namely, Landmark Mobility Private Ltd. This addition will take the total outlet count of Mahindra & Mahindra outlets to 8 in Hyderabad and 12 overall. This expansion is in line with Landmark’s strategy of deepening its engagement within each brand and geography. Such focused growth builds a robust network impact over time and continues to e

Operations & Capacity.pdf_text: Leading Through Innovation March 12, 2026 To To BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Ltd Corporate Relationship Dept., Corporate Relationship Dept., Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Plot No. C/1, G Block, Dalal Street, Mumbai 400001 Bandra-Kurla Complex, Bandra (East), Mumbai 400 051 Scrip Code: 544283 Symbol: ACMESOLAR Reference: Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, as amended Subject: Commissioning of second phase of 33.335 MW/160.51 MWh out of 300 MW/1409.34 MWh capacity of Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) Project located at Village: Badi Sid, Tehsil-Bap, Dist: Phalodi and Jodhpur, Rajasthan by ACME Sun Power Private Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of ACME Solar Holdings Limited Dear Sir/Madam, In continuation of our intimation dated March 06, 2026, we wish to inform that ACME Solar Holdings Limited (“ASHL”), through its wholly owned subsidiary i.e. ACME Sun Power Private Limited, has commissioned the second phase of 33.335 MW/160.51 MWh out of 300 MW/1409.34 MWh capacity of Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) Project located at Village: Badi Sid, Tehsil-Bap, Dist: Phalodi and Jodhpur, Rajasthan. The Commercial operation date (COD) for the stated phase-II shall be March 14, 2026. With this, ACME Sun Power Private Limited has achieved a commissioned capacity of 66.67 MW / 320.99 MWh out of 300 MW/1409.34 MWh. You are requested to take the same on your record. Thanking you, For ACME Solar Holdings Limited Rajesh Sodhi Company Secretary and Compliance Officer ACME Solar Holdings Limited (formerly ACME Solar Holdings Private Limited) CIN : L40106HR2015PLC102129 Regd.. Office : Plot No .152, Sector-44, Gurugram 122002, Haryana, India Tel: +91-124-7117000 Fax : +91-124-7117001 Email : cs.acme@acme.in; Website: www.acmesolar.in;

We request you to kindly take the aforesaid information on record. Thanking You Yours Faithfully, For Natural Capsules Limited Pranjal Deshmukh Company Secretary & Compliance Officer M.No: A66119P RE S S & M E D I A RE L E A S E Natural Biogenex and Fermbox Bio Enter Strategic Framework and Manufacturing Agreements to Strengthen Fermentation-Based CDMO Capabilities Natural Biogenex Private Limited, a subsidiary of Natural Capsules Limited, has executed definitive Framework and Contract Manufacturing Agreements with Fermbox Bio Private Limited to jointly advance fermentation-based Contract Development and Manufacturing (CDMO) services. Under this collaboration, Fermbox will provide advanced fermentation technology and equipment placement of up to ₹60 crore at NBPL’s state-of-the-art facility in Tumkur, while NBPL will undertake licensed manufacturing, quality assurance and regulatory compliance. The partnership positions the Company to serve global biotechnology and biopharma clients with integrated, GMP-compliant manufacturing capacity and enhanced process efficiency. The global biotech and biopharma CDMO market continues to present strong growth opportunities, driven by rising demand, increasing outsourcing, and more complex biomanufacturin

summaries.Fund Raise / QIP / NCD:
## 💰 Fund Raise / QIP / NCD

---

- **Manorama Industries Ltd (541974)** — The board met on March 12, 2026 and approved moving ahead with a fundraise through a QIP (Qualified Institutional Placement), though specific amount and pricing are yet to be disclosed.
📊 **Impact:** Positive (cautiously) — The company is planning to raise fresh money by selling shares to big institutional investors, which can fund growth, but existing shareholders may see their ownership percentage diluted slightly.
👀 **Watch:** The final QIP size and price per share once announced — a steep discount to market price would signal weak demand.

---

- **Artificial Electronics Intelligent Material Ltd (526443)** — The company is awaiting exchange approval for a preferential issue of fully paid-up equity shares through conversion of warrants, following an in-principle application made on June 19, 2025.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This is a procedural update in a long-running process; no new shares have been issued yet and no fresh money has come in.
👀 **Watch:** Whether the exchange grants final approval and how many shares actually get issued upon warrant conversion, as that determines how much existing shareholders get diluted.

---

- **Star Housing Finance Ltd (539017)** — The company has issued a notice exercising the "Put Option" on its Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), meaning bondholders have the right to demand early repayment of their money.
📊 **Impact:** Negative (mild) — If a large number of bondholders ask for their money back early, the company will need to arrange cash quickly, which could strain its finances.
👀 **Watch:** How much NCD money is up for early redemption and whether Star Housing Finance has enough liquidity to repay without borrowing at higher rates.

---

- **Purple Finance Ltd (544191)** — The company disclosed that it has made an interest payment to holders of its Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), confirming timely servicing of its debt obligations.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Paying interest on time is what the company is supposed to do; it shows the company is not defaulting, but this is routine and doesn't move the stock.
👀 **Watch:** Whether all future NCD interest and principal repayment dates are met on schedule, as any miss would be a serious red flag.

---

- **Sadhana Nitro Chem Ltd (506642)** — The Rights Issue Committee of Sadhana Nitro Chem met on March 12, 2026 and approved the allotment of fully paid-up equity shares under its ongoing rights issue.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral to Positive — Completing the allotment means the company has successfully raised the rights issue money from its existing shareholders and can now put it to use.
👀 **Watch:** How the company deploys the funds raised — whether it goes toward reducing debt or funding expansion will determine the long-term benefit to shareholders.

---

- **Bank of India (532149)** — Bank of India's Rights Issue Committee approved the allotment of equity shares under its rights issue on March 12, 2026, completing the share issuance process.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — A public sector bank successfully completing a rights issue means it has strengthened its capital base, making it safer and better positioned to grow its loan book.
👀 **Watch:** The total amount of capital raised and the
summaries.Acquisitions & M&A:
Here are the pre-market bullets for each company in the Acquisitions & M&A section:

---

- **Western Ministil Ltd (504998)** — The company issued a corrigendum (correction notice) to its earlier board meeting outcome dated 12th March 2026 related to an acquisition announcement.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This is just a paperwork correction to a previous announcement; no new deal or money is involved yet, so the stock shouldn't move on this alone.
👀 **Watch:** Whether the corrected filing reveals any change in acquisition price or target company details compared to the original announcement.

---

- **Gravita India Ltd (533282)** — Gravita India has signed definitive agreements to acquire Rashtriya Metal Industries Limited, with specific deal terms disclosed under SEBI Regulation 30.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Gravita, which recycles metals like lead and aluminium, is buying another metals company, which means it could process more material and earn more money in the future.
👀 **Watch:** The acquisition price and whether Rashtriya Metal's capacity meaningfully adds to Gravita's existing recycling volumes.

---

- **JK Lakshmi Cement Ltd (500380)** — The company filed an intimation under SEBI Regulation 30, but the headline does not disclose specific financial details or target names in the available extract.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Without specific deal numbers or a named target in the filing, there is nothing concrete yet that would push the stock up or down.
👀 **Watch:** The actual contents of the full SEBI filing to see if a specific acquisition target or deal value is revealed.

---

- **Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd (532898)** — Power Grid acquired NES Pune East New Transmission Limited under the Tariff Based Competitive Bidding (TBCB) route, adding a new transmission project to its portfolio.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Power Grid won the right to build and operate a new electricity transmission line near Pune, which means steady, government-backed income for decades — like adding a new toll road to its collection.
👀 **Watch:** The project commissioning timeline and the tariff (revenue rate) approved for this transmission line, as that determines how much money it will generate.

---

- **Oxford Industries Ltd (514414)** — A Public Announcement has been submitted to BSE under SEBI Takeover Regulations 3(1), 4, and 15(1), indicating an open offer has been triggered for Oxford Industries Ltd.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Someone is making a formal offer to buy shares from existing shareholders at likely a premium price, which usually causes the stock price to rise toward the offer price.
👀 **Watch:** The open offer price per share announced by Navigant Corporate Advisors, as that becomes the key price level the stock will gravitate toward.

---

- **AGI Infra Ltd (539042)** — The company filed a disclosure under SEBI Regulation 30, but no specific acquisition target, deal size, or financial terms are available in the headline extract.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This appears to be a routine regulatory disclosure; without concrete numbers or a named deal, there is no clear reason for the stock to move.
👀 **Watch:** The full text of the Regulation 30 filing to check if an actual asset purchase or land acquisition with a price tag is buried inside.

---

summaries.Investor Presentations:
I don't have access to the actual documents or filings attached to these announcements — I can only see the headlines, not the contents of the investor presentations themselves.

Since the rule is **"extract actual numbers from the document — do not guess,"** I cannot fabricate revenue figures, order sizes, margins, or growth metrics that I haven't actually read.

---

**What I can do instead — pick one option:**

**Option A — Paste the content**
Share the text/key data points from each presentation (even bullet points or slides summaries), and I'll immediately write the briefing with real numbers.

**Option B — I write with explicit caveats**
I write the bullets using only what the *headline itself* tells us, and flag Line 1 as *"presentation released; specific numbers not yet reviewed"* — fully honest and still useful as a filing tracker.

**Option C — Template with placeholders**
I write the full structure with `[INSERT: revenue figure]`, `[INSERT: order book size]` etc., so you or your team can fill in numbers quickly.

---

**Why this matters for your readers:**
A retail investor briefing with invented numbers is worse than no briefing — it can lead to real financial decisions based on fiction. The honest path protects your credibility.

Which option works for you?

summaries.Operations & Capacity:
• **Chembond Material Technologies Ltd (530871)** — The company has filed an intimation to shareholders regarding the transfer of unclaimed dividend and equity shares to the Investor Education and Protection Fund (IEPF) Authority.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This is a routine legal requirement that every listed company must follow; it simply means some shareholders forgot to claim their dividends, and that money is now being moved to a government fund. No effect on the business or stock price.
👀 **Watch:** Whether any significant number of equity shares are being transferred to IEPF, as a large transfer could indicate poor shareholder engagement by the company over the years.

---

• **ACME Solar Holdings Ltd (ACMESOLAR)** — ACME Solar commissioned the second phase of its Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project in Rajasthan, adding 33.335 MW / 160.51 MWh of capacity out of a total planned 300 MW / 1,409.34 MWh project.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — Think of this like a company opening a new section of its factory; ACME is steadily turning its big energy storage project from a plan on paper into a real, working asset that can earn money.
👀 **Watch:** The pace at which the remaining ~267 MW / ~1,249 MWh of the total BESS capacity gets commissioned, as delays would push back revenue generation from this project.

---

• **Dolphin Offshore Enterprises (DOLPHIN)** — The company disclosed the outcome of a Board of Directors meeting held on March 12, 2026, but the document extract does not contain specific details of any decisions or numbers from that meeting.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — Without knowing what the board actually decided, there is nothing here that clearly helps or hurts the company right now; it is essentially an incomplete filing.
👀 **Watch:** The full board meeting outcome disclosure on the BSE/NSE website to see if any material decisions — such as new contracts, fundraising, or management changes — were approved at this meeting.

---

**Sector Theme:** The ACME Solar announcement is part of a growing wave of energy storage projects coming online across India, as power companies race to store solar and wind energy for use when the sun isn't shining — a trend worth watching across the entire clean energy sector.
